main_dirt.bias_notes: Anti-clockwise oval layout consisting of a unique red sand/dirt surface. Operates exclusively during the dry winter Kimberley season (May–August). Severe on-pace and leader bias ('E' and 'P' pace styles). The heavy red dirt kickback makes it extremely difficult for backmarkers or deep closers to make ground out wide. Horses leading or racing in the 'box seat' along the rail hold a massive advantage.
main_dirt.post_bias: Inside barriers (1–4) are critical across all sprint distances (1000m to 1300m) due to the short home straight, tight turns, and heavy kickback. Wide barriers (8+) are heavily penalized unless the horse possesses elite early speed to lead and cross to the fence immediately.

handicapping_angles.horses_for_courses_specialists: Heavily upgrade 'dirt specialists' with established Broome form or proven aptitude on dirt/sand surfaces (e.g., Port Hedland, Roebourne). Be cautious with grass-only Perth/metropolitan runners trying the red dirt for the first time without dirt trial experience.
handicapping_angles.trainer_jockey_trends: Look for specialized WA country and metropolitan stables shipping horses north for the Broome winter carnival. Front-running riders who aggressively secure early positioning perform exceptionally well here.
